---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 10ed5568e3530a5cb8a74bee92a0faae835023d6
findings:
  - { id: judged-fbl-adj-1, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's archived-match board link is a knowingly dead href: the stub card mints /board/spec/\u003carchived-story\u003e, but boardSpecServer.specDir (internal/workbench/boardspec.go:130) resolves only .verdi/specs/active/, so following the link returns ErrBoardNotFound (404). internal/workbench/directory.go:274 states the posture outright ('the board route serves the working tree's active zone only; an archive-zone spec gets no board link') and deliberately withholds board links for archived specs — yet this story mints exactly such a link. The spec's frozen text says the stub card 'links to the board of every story spec, anywhere in this checkout's store' and co-3 forbids 'a broken link, a 404 affordance'; ac-4 adds 'never an href that 404s when followed'. The e2e file concedes the deviation in a comment (e2e/tests/43-family-board-links.spec.ts, the archived test deliberately does not click through 'so an archived spec's own board route 404s') and resolves the ambiguity in favor of rendering-only compliance inside a test comment rather than the invention ledger. The same hole exists in the ac-1 forward direction: dc-1 declares 'AC-1's forward target likewise resolves from either zone', and attachParentFeatureLink's ix.Get is zone-agnostic, so a story whose parent feature sits in specs/archive/ gets a BoardHref that 404s. Either the archived link needed a servable destination (a sealed read-only archive board) or the card needed the archived state rendered without a board href plus a disclosed reason — minting a 404 affordance satisfies the graph diff while defeating the spec's stated purpose (navigation that replaces URL surgery, never a dead link). (confidence 0.82)" }
  - { id: judged-fbl-adj-2, kind: judged, text: "ac-4's disclosure is silently narrowed to spec-kind targets: attachParentFeatureLink (internal/workbench/familylinks.go:73-76) skips any document-level implements edge whose target fails ParseRef or is not KindSpec, leaving e.g. a dangling implements edge onto an ADR ref (the v0 pattern the code's own comment says stale-decline's document edge uses) as exactly the 'silently inert ref card offering no explanation' the frozen ac-4 text forbids for 'an implements edge whose target does not resolve in the current index' — the frozen criterion is unqualified by target kind. The spec body's 'wherever an edge this story renders' scoping makes this reading defensible, but the narrowing is resolved only in a code comment, not in the invention ledger, and a dangling non-spec implements target on a board now renders indistinguishably from a healthy card. (confidence 0.40)" }
  - { id: judged-fbl-adj-3, kind: judged, text: "On branch-prefixed boards (/b/{branch}/board/spec/...), attachFamilyLinks builds its index over the branch's worktree but mints root-relative hrefs ('/board/' + ref), so a story that resolves only in that branch's store — precisely the drafted-but-unmerged story this feature exists to surface — gets a stub-card link that escapes the /b/{branch}/ prefix and 404s against the serving checkout. This mirrors EditorHref's pre-existing unprefixed posture that dc-2 names as the model, so it inherits rather than invents the defect, but the spec's 'this checkout's store' phrasing plus co-3's no-dead-link constraint arguably required prefix-aware hrefs once the enrichment is served under the branch mount. (confidence 0.30)" }
  - { id: judged-fbl-adj-4, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's 'every story spec' is implemented as 'every backlink source with an implemented-by edge' (matchingStoryRefs, internal/workbench/familylinks.go:147-163) with no class check on the matched ref — any non-story spec (or future kind) whose implements edge names a stub's AC would be linked as a matched story. This matches dex's implementingStoryRefs posture exactly as dc-1 demands ('the SAME primitive the SAME way'), so it is aligned with the declared mechanism; noted only because the frozen ac text says 'story spec' and the mechanism enforces no such restriction. (confidence 0.15)" }
digest: sha256:6811112d0f6a449777ed28011bfe05dc03896ba1269c76ff907100f45b6cc7b7
integrity: sha256:d63dcd94e5739eae552239d9db188838e6f884eb6fdb6bb613d95d96bd1733cf
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\n  \"findings\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"fbl-adj-1\",\n      \"text\": \"ac-2's archived-match board link is a knowingly dead href: the stub card mints /board/spec/\u003carchived-story\u003e, but boardSpecServer.specDir (internal/workbench/boardspec.go:130) resolves only .verdi/specs/active/, so following the link returns ErrBoardNotFound (404). internal/workbench/directory.go:274 states the posture outright ('the board route serves the working tree's active zone only; an archive-zone spec gets no board link') and deliberately withholds board links for archived specs — yet this story mints exactly such a link. The spec's frozen text says the stub card 'links to the board of every story spec, anywhere in this checkout's store' and co-3 forbids 'a broken link, a 404 affordance'; ac-4 adds 'never an href that 404s when followed'. The e2e file concedes the deviation in a comment (e2e/tests/43-family-board-links.spec.ts, the archived test deliberately does not click through 'so an archived spec's own board route 404s') and resolves the ambiguity in favor of rendering-only compliance inside a test comment rather than the invention ledger. The same hole exists in the ac-1 forward direction: dc-1 declares 'AC-1's forward target likewise resolves from either zone', and attachParentFeatureLink's ix.Get is zone-agnostic, so a story whose parent feature sits in specs/archive/ gets a BoardHref that 404s. Either the archived link needed a servable destination (a sealed read-only archive board) or the card needed the archived state rendered without a board href plus a disclosed reason — minting a 404 affordance satisfies the graph diff while defeating the spec's stated purpose (navigation that replaces URL surgery, never a dead link).\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.82\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"fbl-adj-2\",\n      \"text\": \"ac-4's disclosure is silently narrowed to spec-kind targets: attachParentFeatureLink (internal/workbench/familylinks.go:73-76) skips any document-level implements edge whose target fails ParseRef or is not KindSpec, leaving e.g. a dangling implements edge onto an ADR ref (the v0 pattern the code's own comment says stale-decline's document edge uses) as exactly the 'silently inert ref card offering no explanation' the frozen ac-4 text forbids for 'an implements edge whose target does not resolve in the current index' — the frozen criterion is unqualified by target kind. The spec body's 'wherever an edge this story renders' scoping makes this reading defensible, but the narrowing is resolved only in a code comment, not in the invention ledger, and a dangling non-spec implements target on a board now renders indistinguishably from a healthy card.\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.4\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"fbl-adj-3\",\n      \"text\": \"On branch-prefixed boards (/b/{branch}/board/spec/...), attachFamilyLinks builds its index over the branch's worktree but mints root-relative hrefs ('/board/' + ref), so a story that resolves only in that branch's store — precisely the drafted-but-unmerged story this feature exists to surface — gets a stub-card link that escapes the /b/{branch}/ prefix and 404s against the serving checkout. This mirrors EditorHref's pre-existing unprefixed posture that dc-2 names as the model, so it inherits rather than invents the defect, but the spec's 'this checkout's store' phrasing plus co-3's no-dead-link constraint arguably required prefix-aware hrefs once the enrichment is served under the branch mount.\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.3\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"fbl-adj-4\",\n      \"text\": \"ac-2's 'every story spec' is implemented as 'every backlink source with an implemented-by edge' (matchingStoryRefs, internal/workbench/familylinks.go:147-163) with no class check on the matched ref — any non-story spec (or future kind) whose implements edge names a stub's AC would be linked as a matched story. This matches dex's implementingStoryRefs posture exactly as dc-1 demands ('the SAME primitive the SAME way'), so it is aligned with the declared mechanism; noted only because the frozen ac text says 'story spec' and the mechanism enforces no such restriction.\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.15\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/family-board-links@10ed5568e3530a5cb8a74bee92a0faae835023d6, spec/family-board-links@24970932f5164bba3912d26b7bff05ad5ba8a94c], digest: sha256:6811112d0f6a449777ed28011bfe05dc03896ba1269c76ff907100f45b6cc7b7, integrity: sha256:d63dcd94e5739eae552239d9db188838e6f884eb6fdb6bb613d95d96bd1733cf }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-fbl-adj-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-2's archived-match board link is a knowingly dead href: the stub card mints /board/spec/<archived-story>, but boardSpecServer.specDir (internal/workbench/boardspec.go:130) resolves only .verdi/specs/active/, so following the link returns ErrBoardNotFound (404). internal/workbench/directory.go:274 states the posture outright ('the board route serves the working tree's active zone only; an archive-zone spec gets no board link') and deliberately withholds board links for archived specs — yet this story mints exactly such a link. The spec's frozen text says the stub card 'links to the board of every story spec, anywhere in this checkout's store' and co-3 forbids 'a broken link, a 404 affordance'; ac-4 adds 'never an href that 404s when followed'. The e2e file concedes the deviation in a comment (e2e/tests/43-family-board-links.spec.ts, the archived test deliberately does not click through 'so an archived spec's own board route 404s') and resolves the ambiguity in favor of rendering-only compliance inside a test comment rather than the invention ledger. The same hole exists in the ac-1 forward direction: dc-1 declares 'AC-1's forward target likewise resolves from either zone', and attachParentFeatureLink's ix.Get is zone-agnostic, so a story whose parent feature sits in specs/archive/ gets a BoardHref that 404s. Either the archived link needed a servable destination (a sealed read-only archive board) or the card needed the archived state rendered without a board href plus a disclosed reason — minting a 404 affordance satisfies the graph diff while defeating the spec's stated purpose (navigation that replaces URL surgery, never a dead link). (confidence 0.82)
- **judged-fbl-adj-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-4's disclosure is silently narrowed to spec-kind targets: attachParentFeatureLink (internal/workbench/familylinks.go:73-76) skips any document-level implements edge whose target fails ParseRef or is not KindSpec, leaving e.g. a dangling implements edge onto an ADR ref (the v0 pattern the code's own comment says stale-decline's document edge uses) as exactly the 'silently inert ref card offering no explanation' the frozen ac-4 text forbids for 'an implements edge whose target does not resolve in the current index' — the frozen criterion is unqualified by target kind. The spec body's 'wherever an edge this story renders' scoping makes this reading defensible, but the narrowing is resolved only in a code comment, not in the invention ledger, and a dangling non-spec implements target on a board now renders indistinguishably from a healthy card. (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-fbl-adj-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: On branch-prefixed boards (/b/{branch}/board/spec/...), attachFamilyLinks builds its index over the branch's worktree but mints root-relative hrefs ('/board/' + ref), so a story that resolves only in that branch's store — precisely the drafted-but-unmerged story this feature exists to surface — gets a stub-card link that escapes the /b/{branch}/ prefix and 404s against the serving checkout. This mirrors EditorHref's pre-existing unprefixed posture that dc-2 names as the model, so it inherits rather than invents the defect, but the spec's 'this checkout's store' phrasing plus co-3's no-dead-link constraint arguably required prefix-aware hrefs once the enrichment is served under the branch mount. (confidence 0.30)
- **judged-fbl-adj-4** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-2's 'every story spec' is implemented as 'every backlink source with an implemented-by edge' (matchingStoryRefs, internal/workbench/familylinks.go:147-163) with no class check on the matched ref — any non-story spec (or future kind) whose implements edge names a stub's AC would be linked as a matched story. This matches dex's implementingStoryRefs posture exactly as dc-1 demands ('the SAME primitive the SAME way'), so it is aligned with the declared mechanism; noted only because the frozen ac text says 'story spec' and the mechanism enforces no such restriction. (confidence 0.15)
